title: Shashank Chaddha
content:
People.ContactEmail schaddha@mccarthy.ca
- Western Law, Ivey Business School
Shashank obtained his Juris Doctor at Western Law in April 2025. Prior to Western Law, he earned his Bachelor of Laws and Bachelor of Arts (integrated degree) in India.
While at Western, Shashank was actively involved with the Western Business Law Clinic. He also participated in the Torys LLP Negotiation Competition (where he placed second) and went on to represent the law school at the Canadian National Negotiation Competition in Montréal.
In the summer after his first year, he was selected as the McCarthy Tétrault LLP Business Law Intern to work with CIBC, where he took on a variety of assignments involving retail and commercial banking.
Last year, Shashank presented his research paper titled “Same-Sex Marriage: Upholding Minority Rights versus Judicial Caution” at the 17th Annual Canadian Law Student Conference hosted by the University of Windsor.
During his final year, he went on exchange to Italy and attended the 69th session of the Commission on the Status of Women at the United Nations Headquarters in New York in March 2025. He also participated in the 2025 Walsh Family Negotiation Moot held in Toronto.
Outside of academics and work, Shashank is deeply invested in fostering inclusivity and awareness within the LGBTQ+ community. As a first-generation immigrant, he finds fulfillment in connecting with fellow immigrants, always striving to embody empathy and understanding in his interactions. In his spare time, he enjoys roller-blading, culinary exploration, and re-watching FRIENDS.
